title: Aggregation and comparison of multi-tab content
abstract: Methods, systems, and apparatus for facilitating a comparison of content in a user interface are described. Two or more open browser interfaces of a browser are identified, each of the two or more open browser interfaces containing an item description of one or more items. The browser interfaces include at least one of a tab of the browser or a window of the browser. One or more features related to each of the one or more items from at least two of the item descriptions are extracted or identified. The comparison for a plurality of items is generated based on the extracted or identified one or more features. The comparison of the plurality of items is displayed within at least one of the browser interfaces.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus for facilitating a compari son of content in a user interface, the apparatus comprising: one or more hardware processors; memory to store instructions that, when executed by the one or more hardware processors, cause the one or more hardware processors to perform operations comprising: identifying two or more open browser interfaces of a browser, each of the two or more open browser interfaces containing an item description of one or more items, wherein the browser interfaces include at least one of: a tab of the browser or a window of the browser; extracting or identifying one or more features related to each of the one or more items from at least two of the item descriptions, generating the comparison for a plurality of items based on the extracted or identified one or more features; and displaying, within at least one of the browser interfaces, the comparison of the plurality of items. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, the operations further comprising selecting two or more browser interfaces associated with a same URL domain for use in generating the comparison. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, the operations further comprising selecting two or more browser interfaces based on a type of item described on the two or more browser interfaces. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, the operations further comprising displaying the comparison as an overlay on at least one of the two or more open browser interfaces. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, the operations further comprising displaying the comparison as a table on another open browser interface of the browser. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, the operations further comprising performing text analysi s on one of the item descriptions to determine an identifi cation of the item or the one or more features of the item. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein an identification of the item or the one or more features are extracted from metadata associated with one of the item descriptions. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, the operations further comprising identifying the one or more features by identifying one or more semantic topics of one of the item descriptions. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, the operations further comprising obtaining a corresponding list of key features of the item and searching one of the item descriptions for one or more features on the list of key features. 10. An method for facilitating a comparison of content in a user interface, the method comprising: identifying, using one or more hardware processors, two or more open browser interfaces of a browser, each of the two or more open browser interfaces containing an item description of one or more items, wherein the browser interfaces include at least one of a tab of the browser or a window of the browser; extracting or identifying, using at least one of the one or more hardware processors, one or more features related to each of the one or more items from at least two of the item descriptions; generating the comparison for a plurality of items based on the extracted or identified one or more features, and displaying, within at least one of the browser interfaces, the comparison of the plurality of items. 1 1. The method of claim 10, further comprising identifying the one or more features by identifying one or more semantic topics of one of the item descriptions. 18. The method of claim 10, further comprising obtaining a corresponding list of key features of the item and searching one of the item descriptions for one or more features on the list of key features. 19.
